errors: The ELM implementation does not account for the 20 pack-year smoking history as recommended by the USPSTF. The "Has Smoking History" logic definition only checks for the presence of a tobacco use condition, but does not consider the quantity or duration of smoking. Additionally, the ELM implementation does not specify the pack-year history in the rationale or recommendation.

The age thresholds in the ELM implementation (Age >= 60 years and Age <= 75 years) do not match the typical clinical guidelines for colorectal cancer screening, which usually recommend screening for average-risk individuals starting at age 50 and continuing until at least age 75, with some guidelines suggesting screening up to age 85 or beyond, depending on the individual's health status and life expectancy. The lookback period of 2 years for recent colorectal screening may also not align with all clinical guidelines, as the recommended screening interval can vary based on the screening modality and individual risk factors.

The age thresholds in the ELM implementation (30-65 years) do not match the recommended age range in the clinical practice guideline (21-65 years). The ELM implementation should be updated to reflect the correct age range.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation is 75 years, but the USPSTF recommendation is for adults 65 years or older. This discrepancy may lead to patients between 65-74 years being missed for falls prevention screening.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation is 25 years, but the USPSTF recommends screening for unhealthy alcohol use in adults 18 years or older. This discrepancy may lead to missed screenings for younger adults.

The ELM implementation specifies an age range of 55-75 years, but the USPSTF recommendation in the rationale section specifies an age range of 65-75 years for one-time AAA screening with ultrasonography in men who have ever smoked. This discrepancy indicates a potential mismatch between the ELM and the clinical practice guideline (CPG).

The ELM implementation does not fully align with the clinical practice guidelines (CPG). Specifically, the age threshold in the ELM is set at 50 years, but the CPG recommends annual LDCT for lung cancer screening in adults aged 50-80 years. Additionally, the ELM does not account for the 20 pack-year smoking history recommended by the USPSTF. The lookback period for recent LDCT is 1 year, which may be reasonable, but the ELM does not specify the pack-year history or the upper age limit of 80 years as recommended by the CPG.
